bibliographic_data.title: The Tangent Search Engine: Improved Similarity Metrics and Scalability for Math Formula Search
bibliographic_data.abstract: With the ever-increasing quantity and variety of data worldwide, the Web has become a rich repository of mathematical formulae. This necessitates the creation of robust and scalable systems for Mathematical Information Retrieval (MIR), where users search for mathematical information using individual formulae or a combination of keywords and formulae. Often, the pages that best satisfy users' information needs contain expressions that only approximately match the query formulae. For users trying to locate or re-find a specific expression, browse for similar formulae, or who are mathematical non-experts, the similarity of formulae depends more on the relative positions of symbols than on deep mathematical semantics. We propose the Maximum Subtree Similarity (MSS) metric for query-by-expression that produces intuitive rankings of formulae based on their appearance, as represented by the types and relative positions of symbols. Because it is too expensive to apply the metric against all formulae in large collections, we first retrieve expressions using an inverted index over tuples that encode relationships between pairs of symbols, ranking hits using the Dice coefficient. The top-k formulae are then re-ranked using MSS. Our approach obtains state-of-the-art performance on the NTCIR-11 Wikipedia formula retrieval benchmark and is efficient in terms of both index space and overall retrieval time.

core_content.novelty_claim: The paper proposes the Maximum Subtree Similarity (MSS) metric for query-by-expression, which ranks formulae based on their appearance and relative positions of symbols.
core_content.key_methodology: The method involves using an inverted index over tuples representing pairs of symbols to quickly retrieve candidate results, followed by re-ranking these candidates with MSS. The system architecture includes a two-level cascading search system for scalability.
outcomes: Obtains state-of-the-art performance on the NTCIR-11 Wikipedia formula retrieval benchmark
Efficient in terms of both index space and overall retrieval time
core_content.full_text_markdown: The Tangent Search Engine is an innovative approach to Mathematical Information Retrieval (MIR) that addresses the challenge of searching for mathematical expressions. The system uses a two-level cascading search method, where the first level employs an inverted index over symbol pairs and the second level re-ranks results using the Maximum Subtree Similarity (MSS) metric. MSS is designed to rank formulae based on their visual structure, making it particularly useful for non-experts or when exact matches are not available. The system's efficiency and effectiveness have been demonstrated through its performance on benchmarks like NTCIR-11.
